name: Prostamax
subtitle: KEDP Tetrapeptide | Prostate Bioregulator
overview: Prostamax is a Khavinson bioregulator tetrapeptide (KEDP) with primary repair effects on prostate tissue. Developed by Professor Vladimir Khavinson at the St. Petersburg Institute of Bioregulation and Gerontology, it alters chromatin structure in cells from elderly individuals, promoting deheterochromatinization and potentially reactivating genes repressed during aging. Research in rat models shows reduced prostate inflammation, decreased swelling, and decelerated pathological remodeling associated with prostatitis.
mechanism: Prostamax works through epigenetic regulation by altering chromatin structure. It increases the frequency of sister chromatid exchanges and Ag-positive nucleolar organizer regions (NORs), while reducing large segments of pericentromeric heterochromatin. These changes promote chromatin decondensation and deheterochromatinization, potentially reactivating genes repressed during aging. The peptide influences heterochromatin arrangements in human lymphocytes and normalizes age-related changes in cellular function.

items.name: Chronic Prostatitis
items.description: Research shows reduced inflammation and swelling in prostatitis models.

items.name: Prostatic Inflammation
items.description: Mitigates inflammation and immune cell infiltration in prostate tissue.

items.name: Prostate Tissue Repair
items.description: Decreases scarring and pathological remodeling in prostate.

items.name: Gene Reactivation
items.description: Potentially reactivates genes repressed during aging process.

items.name: Lymphocyte Function
items.description: Normalizes age-related changes in lymphocyte function.

deliveryMethods.overview: Available in capsule form for oral administration. Short peptides can be absorbed orally and reach target tissues. Typical protocol involves 10-20 day cycles.
Injectable form available for direct administration.

interactions.notes: Complementary male reproductive bioregulators; different tissue targets.
Often combined in comprehensive anti-aging Khavinson protocols.
Different organ targets; can be used in comprehensive bioregulator protocols.
Part of Khavinson bioregulator family; targets different tissue.

timeline.effects: Chromatin remodeling and gene expression changes begin
Effects persist due to epigenetic changes

monitoring: PSA testing recommended for men over 50

quickStats.storage: Capsules at room temperature; reconstituted injectable at 2-8°C refrigerated
